specs.feature: Track Changes engine for the Review tab (Tracking group + Changes group), plus the dependent Comments-group review commands. When Track Changes is ON, every edit becomes a recorded revision: typed/pasted text is wrapped in <ins class="wc-ins">, deleted text is wrapped in <del class="wc-del"> (kept, not removed), and formatting changes are flagged. Four Display for Review modes (Simple Markup / All Markup / No Markup / Original) re-render the same DOM via CSS classes on #editor. Show Markup submenu toggles Comments / Insertions and Deletions / Formatting / Balloons / Specific People. Accept / Accept All / Accept and Move to Next and Reject / Reject All commands plus Previous/Next change navigation, and a Reviewing Pane (vertical or horizontal) listing every revision. Persisted in the saved HTML (Word keeps unaccepted changes in the file).
specs.wordBehavior:
Confirmed Microsoft behavior to clone (from support.microsoft.com + screen-reader docs):

DISPLAY FOR REVIEW (the combo in the Tracking group, 4 modes):
- Simple Markup (DEFAULT): hides inline ins/del markup; shows ONLY a vertical "changed line" (a red bar) in the LEFT margin next to any paragraph that contains a change. Comments collapse to balloon hints. Word's default review view.
- All Markup: shows every insertion (colored underline) and deletion (colored strikethrough) inline, plus the change bar, plus formatting-change notes and comment balloons.
- No Markup: shows the FINAL document as if all changes were accepted (insertions shown as normal text, deletions hidden) WITHOUT actually accepting them. Nothing is removed from the file.
- Original: shows the ORIGINAL document as if all changes were rejected (deletions shown as normal text, insertions hidden).

MARKUP COLORS: Default setting is "By author." The first/only author's INSERTIONS render as underlined text in that author's color; DELETIONS render as strikethrough in the same color. Word's default first-author color is RED. Additional authors cycle through distinct colors (blue, green, etc.). The change line bar in the margin is also colored (red by default). Formatting changes are noted (balloon or change bar) but text is not struck.

SHOW MARKUP submenu (filter what is visible; toggles checked by default): Comments; Insertions and Deletions; Formatting; Balloons (sub-options: Show Revisions in Balloons / Show All Revisions Inline / Show Only Formatting in Balloons); Specific People (Reviewers list -> All Reviewers or pick individual authors). Toggling a type hides/shows that markup category without accepting/rejecting it.

CHANGES group commands (exact labels): Accept split -> Accept and Move to Next / Accept This Change / Accept All Changes Shown / Accept All Changes / Accept All Changes and Stop Tracking. Reject split -> Reject and Move to Next / Reject This Change / Reject All Changes Shown / Reject All Changes / Reject All Changes and Stop Tracking. Previous / Next jump between tracked changes. Accept = keep the change permanently (insertion text stays as normal text; deletion text removed). Reject = undo (insertion removed; deletion text restored as normal). 'and Stop Tracking' variants also turn Track Changes off.

REVIEWING PANE (split): two orientations -- Reviewing Pane Vertical (beside doc) and Reviewing Pane Horizontal (below doc). Titled 'Revisions'. Lists every comment + tracked change selected in Show Markup, each with author + change type + content, and a summary count at top. Clicking an entry scrolls to that change.

TRACK CHANGES split: main toggles tracking on/off (Ctrl+Shift+E); dropdown also has Lock Tracking... (password-gated; document only / optional simple impl).

ACCESSIBILITY: Screen readers announce 'change' at the start of any changed paragraph (Simple Markup) and announce change + change type at the change location (All Markup); formatting changes are read out. Clone: use native <ins>/<del> (semantic), add aria-label/title with author + 'inserted'/'deleted' + text, mark changed paragraphs, never rely on color alone (underline for ins, strikethrough for del satisfy this).

COMPARE group = cloud/no (Compare / Combine legal blackline) -- document as not implemented. Protect group (Block Authors = no, Restrict Editing = partial) out of scope.
specs.webFindings:
All behavior confirmed from primary Microsoft sources plus corroborating guides:
- Track changes in Word (official): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/track-changes-in-word-197ba630-0f5f-4a8e-9a77-3712475e806a -- 4 Display for Review modes, Show Markup submenu, Accept/Reject variants (incl. and Move to Next, All Changes, and Stop Tracking), Previous/Next, Reviewing Pane vertical/horizontal, Lock Tracking with password.
- Change the track changes color (official): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/change-the-track-changes-color-48c80bb3-458c-4abb-8e21-25f9bac47484 -- default By author; first author insertions = colored underline, deletions = colored strikethrough; Word picks red first; per-author colors may shift on reopen.
- Use a screen reader to track and review changes (official, accessibility): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/use-a-screen-reader-to-track-and-review-changes-in-a-document-in-word-8d415281-6ef2-41ea-8532-38e410be5988 -- Simple Markup announces 'change' at start of changed paragraph; All Markup announces change + type at location; formatting read by JAWS.
- Corroborating: erinwrightwriting.com (Show Markup balloons + reviewers), style.mla.org and papercheck.com (Simple Markup = red margin line, no struck text; All Markup = colored text/lines per reviewer), customguide/geeksforgeeks (command labels).

No new external APIs required. Cloud Review features to merely document (not build): Compare/Combine (legal blackline diff), Block Authors (SharePoint/OneDrive co-authoring), and existing Translate/Researcher/Linked Notes already toasted as not-implemented.
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes, UI is needed but it reuses existing primitives -- no new framework. All Review-tab ribbon controls already exist in ribbon-data.js (Tracking, Changes, Comments, Compare, Protect groups) with correct cmd ids and split/combo/dropdown types; they currently fall through to notImplemented. UI pieces to build:

1. CSS-only render of the 4 display modes via classes on #editor (.tc-mode-simple / .tc-mode-all / .tc-mode-nomarkup / .tc-mode-original) -- no DOM rewrite to switch modes.
2. Inline markup styling: .wc-ins (colored underline) and .wc-del (colored strikethrough); author color via inline color or data-author->CSS-var mapping.
3. Left-margin change bar: border-left/::before on changed blocks (.wc-changed), red by default, shown in Simple + All markup, hidden in No Markup/Original.
4. Flyout menus for the split/dropdown buttons (Accept dropdown, Reject dropdown, Show Markup submenu, Reviewing Pane vertical/horizontal, Track Changes dropdown with Lock Tracking) -- built with existing WC.flyout/flyItem/flyHeader/flySep, like every other tab.
5. Display for Review combo (type:combo) wired through Commands.comboCommit/comboDropdown (currently only font/fontSize) to set the mode; check-mark the active mode.
6. Reviewing Pane: a task pane reusing existing .taskpane/.tp-head/.tp-body markup (same pattern as navPane/stylesPane in dialogs.js), appended to #workarea, with a class toggling vertical (side) vs horizontal (bottom) layout, listing each ins/del/comment node with author + type + text and a summary count; clicking scrolls to and selects the node. Add minimal CSS for .reviewing-pane.horizontal.

No images/assets needed; icons come from the existing WC.icon set already used by the ribbon.
specs.implementationPlan:
Create one new module src/renderer/js/track-changes.js (WC.TrackChanges) wired in index.html after commands.js, plus edits to editor.js, commands.js, and editor.css. Keep WC.trackChanges boolean as the single source of truth (already exists).

DATA MODEL (DOM-native, persists in getHTML):
- Insertion: <ins class="wc-ins" data-author="Me" data-date="ISO" title="Inserted by Me">text</ins>
- Deletion: <del class="wc-del" data-author="Me" data-date="ISO" title="Deleted by Me">text</del>
- Format change (lightweight): wrap run in <span class="wc-fmt" data-author title="Formatting change"> + change bar; full attribute diffing out of scope -- flag + note only.
- Author/color: WC.TrackChanges.author='Me'. Maintain author->color map; first author = red (#C00000-ish). Apply color inline so it survives save, or via [data-author] CSS rules.

1. EDITOR INTERCEPTION (editor.js, new beforeinput handler on this.node, active only when WC.trackChanges):
   - Add this.node.addEventListener('beforeinput', (e)=>WC.TrackChanges.onBeforeInput(e)) in init(). Return immediately if !WC.trackChanges.
   - insertText/insertCompositionText/insertParagraph/insertFromPaste/insertReplacementText: e.preventDefault(), insert typed data wrapped in fresh-or-current <ins.wc-ins> at caret (merge into adjacent same-author <ins> at its boundary so continuous typing stays in one node). Use direct DOM insertion (range.insertNode) like Editor.insertNodeHTML to preserve classes/attrs -- NOT execCommand('insertHTML') which strips them under styleWithCSS. Move caret inside the ins.
   - deleteContentBackward/Forward/deleteByCut/deleteWordBackward etc.: e.preventDefault(). Compute range (collapsed -> extend one grapheme in delete direction; non-collapsed -> selection). For each run: text inside a fresh <ins> by SAME author is hard-removed; everything else is WRAPPED in <del.wc-del> (split text nodes at edges, surroundContents). Collapse caret. Step over text already in a <del> (no double-delete).
   - Formatting commands while tracking: wrap touched run in .wc-fmt + mark block changed (minimal: bar + note, no full style diff).
   - Mark containing block(s) .wc-changed whenever ins/del/fmt created (drives margin bar). Recompute on accept/reject.
   - IME: compositionstart/compositionend flags; wrap final composed text on compositionend, not per-keystroke.

2. COMMANDS (commands.js, replace placeholder H.trackChanges and add handlers; branch shared cmds on control.id):
   - H.trackChanges(c,node): main id -> WC.TrackChanges.toggle(node). Dropdown (Commands.dropdown 'trackChanges') -> flyout: 'Track Changes' (toggle) + 'Lock Tracking...' (WC.TrackChanges.lockDialog()).
   - displayForReview is type:combo -> extend comboCommit/comboDropdown: comboDropdown opens a flyout of the 4 modes with a check on active; select -> WC.TrackChanges.setMode(mode) + update combo input. comboCommit accepts a typed/selected mode string.
   - H.showMarkup (dropdown) -> flyout with checkable items: Comments, Insertions and Deletions, Formatting, Balloons (submenu), Specific People (submenu: All Reviewers + each author), Reviewing Pane. Each toggles a boolean in WC.TrackChanges.show{} and calls applyShow() toggling #editor classes (.hide-ins-del/.hide-formatting/.hide-comments) + per-author visibility.
   - H.reviewingPane (split): main -> reviewingPane('vertical'); dropdown -> Vertical / Horizontal.
   - H.accept (split): main = Accept and Move to Next; dropdown -> 5 variants mapping to WC.TrackChanges.accept(current)/acceptAndNext()/acceptAllShown()/acceptAll()/acceptAllStopTracking(). H.reject mirrors.
   - Changes-group Previous/Next (cmd 'previous'/'next', control.id = review.changes.previous/next): in H.previous/H.next branch on control.id -- review.changes.* -> WC.TrackChanges.prev()/next(); review.comments.* -> WC.Comments.prev()/next(). Same for H.delete (review.comments.delete -> delete comment(s)) and H.showComments.
   - controlIndex cmd-collision: ribbon.js keys controlIndex by c.cmd first-wins, so next/previous/delete collide; handlers must use the control arg passed to run() (correct one), NOT controlIndex.

3. ACCEPT/REJECT engine (track-changes.js):
   - accept(node): <ins> -> unwrap (text permanent); <del> -> remove; .wc-fmt -> unwrap keep style.
   - reject(node): <ins> -> remove; <del> -> unwrap (restore text); .wc-fmt -> unwrap/revert (minimal unwrap).
   - acceptAll/rejectAll iterate all ins/del/fmt (or only Show-filtered for 'Shown'). ...StopTracking also set WC.trackChanges=false + untoggle button.
   - After any op: recompute .wc-changed, normalize adjacent text nodes, refresh pane + Next/Prev cursor, Editor.dirty=true, repaginate, emit.
   - Current-change pointer set by Prev/Next or caret; default first change.

4. NAVIGATION: next()/prev() build ordered list querySelectorAll('ins.wc-ins,del.wc-del,.wc-fmt') in document order, filtered by Show settings; move cursor, scrollIntoView, select/highlight via a Range. Wrap-around like Word.

5. DISPLAY MODES (editor.css, switched by setMode adding one class on #editor):
   - .tc-mode-all: ins underline+color, del strikethrough+color shown, change bars + notes/balloons visible.
   - .tc-mode-simple: del hidden, ins shown as normal text, change bar shown, comments as hint markers.
   - .tc-mode-nomarkup: del{display:none}; ins markup stripped (normal text); bars/comments hidden = final doc.
   - .tc-mode-original: ins{display:none}; del shown as normal text; bars/comments hidden = original doc.
   - Change bar: .wc-changed border-left:3px solid #C00 with negative margin into page margin (or ::before). Hidden in nomarkup/original.

6. PERSISTENCE: getHTML already clones and strips only guides/find-hits -- ins/del/wc-changed survive into saved files (correct; Word keeps unaccepted changes). setHTML -> call WC.TrackChanges.onDocLoad() to recompute author colors + change bars + reset nav cursor (add a hook beside the existing WC.Draw.onDocLoad() call in Editor.setHTML).

7. WIRING: add <script src="js/track-changes.js"> in index.html before app.js (after commands.js). WC.TrackChanges.init() from app.js init (or self-init on DOMContentLoaded). WC.trackChanges stays the on/off flag the engine reads.

OUT OF SCOPE / DOCUMENT AS NOT IMPLEMENTED (toast + docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md): Compare/Combine (legal blackline), Block Authors (cloud co-authoring), full per-attribute Formatting diff in balloons, Lock Tracking password enforcement beyond a basic prompt, and existing cloud items (Translate, Researcher, Linked Notes).
specs.testPlan:
No automated test harness in the repo (vanilla JS Electron app; manual/visual verification is the norm). Verify manually by launching the app (/run skill / Electron) and exercising:

ENGINE (Track Changes ON via Tracking button, Ctrl+Shift+E):
- Type mid-paragraph -> colored underlined insertion wrapped in <ins class="wc-ins"> (inspect DOM); continuous typing stays one ins.
- Select existing text, press Delete/Backspace -> text NOT removed; becomes colored strikethrough <del class="wc-del">; caret lands correctly.
- Delete your own just-typed insertion -> hard-removed (no del), matching Word.
- Backspace/Delete across a word and a paragraph boundary -> wrapped, no DOM corruption, structure intact.
- Apply bold/color while tracking -> run flagged (.wc-fmt) + change bar appears.
- Turn OFF -> typing/deleting behaves normally (no ins/del).

DISPLAY MODES (Display for Review combo):
- All Markup: ins underlined, del struck, both colored, margin change bar.
- Simple Markup: only margin change bar; struck text hidden, insertions read normal.
- No Markup: reads as accepted (ins normal, del gone) but DOM still has ins/del.
- Original: reads as rejected (del present as normal text, ins hidden).
- Cycle all 4 and back -> no revision lost.

SHOW MARKUP:
- Insertions and Deletions off -> inline markup hidden, bar may remain.
- Formatting off -> .wc-fmt notes hidden.
- Specific People -> uncheck an author -> their changes hidden; All Reviewers re-shows.

CHANGES group:
- Next/Previous cycle every change in document order, scroll + highlight, wrap around.
- Accept This Change: insertion -> permanent (ins unwrapped); deletion -> text removed.
- Reject This Change: insertion -> removed; deletion -> text restored.
- Accept and Move to Next advances cursor.
- Accept All / Reject All clears all markup to correct final/original text; ...and Stop Tracking also turns button off.
- 'Shown' variants act only on currently-visible (filtered) changes.

REVIEWING PANE:
- Vertical -> lists every change with author + type + text + summary count; click scrolls to it.
- Horizontal -> renders below the document.
- Accept/Reject updates list + count live.

COMMENTS group regression (shared next/previous/delete cmd ids):
- New Comment works; Previous/Next/Delete in the Comments group operate on COMMENTS, not tracked changes (verify control.id branching).

PERSISTENCE:
- Save (getHTML) then reload (setHTML) -> ins/del/change bars/author colors survive; nav + pane rebuild.

ACCESSIBILITY spot-check:
- ins/del are real <ins>/<del> with title/aria-label stating author + inserted/deleted; markup distinguishable without color (underline vs strikethrough).
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible and a strong fit for this codebase. The Review-tab ribbon controls already exist in ribbon-data.js with the exact Microsoft labels and the right control types (split/combo/dropdown), and they currently fall through to notImplemented, so there are no conflicting handlers to unwind. The editor is a single contenteditable with clean extension points: there is no beforeinput listener yet (the input listener is only a debounced repaginate), so intercepting beforeinput to wrap insertions in <ins> and deletions in <del> is a clean, additive change. Existing WC.Editor helpers (insertNodeHTML for class/attr-preserving DOM insertion, saveRange/restoreRange, selectedBlocks, getHTML/setHTML) cover the needed primitives. getHTML already preserves arbitrary markup (only guides/find-hits stripped), so revisions persist in saved files exactly as Word does. The Reviewing Pane reuses the proven .taskpane pattern (navPane/stylesPane). WC.trackChanges already exists as the on/off flag.

Real engineering risks, all manageable: (1) beforeinput across IME/composition -- guard with compositionstart/end and a whitelist of inputTypes, falling back to native input + post-processing if a type is unhandled. (2) Splitting/merging text nodes when wrapping deletions and merging consecutive insertions needs careful Range math to avoid nesting ins inside del or corrupting the DOM; bounded with surroundContents + splitText + normalize. (3) The cmd-collision on next/previous/delete (shared between Comments and Changes groups; controlIndex keys first-wins by cmd) means handlers must branch on control.id from the passed control, not controlIndex -- a documented gotcha, not a blocker. (4) Full Formatting-change diffing (Word shows old->new attributes in balloons) is the only genuinely heavy part and is intentionally scoped to a flag + bar + note. (5) Lock Tracking password enforcement, Compare/Combine, and Block Authors are correctly left as cloud/not-implemented. Net: core insertion/deletion tracking, accept/reject, navigation, four display modes, Show Markup filters, and the Reviewing Pane are all buildable with existing primitives in one new module plus targeted edits to editor.js, commands.js, and editor.css.

specs.feature: Review > Comments group: New Comment, Delete (split: Delete / Delete All Comments Shown / Delete All Comments in Document), Previous, Next, Show Comments (toggle). Upgrade the existing stub WC.Comments.add into a real comment system with a data model, a right-side comments pane (cards with author/timestamp/text), anchor highlighting in the document, Previous/Next navigation, single + bulk delete, and a Show Comments toggle. Cloud-only relatives (Translate, Researcher, Linked Notes) are out of scope and documented as not implemented.
specs.wordBehavior:
Microsoft Word "modern comments" behavior (verified via Microsoft Support):

NEW COMMENT: Select text (or place caret), then Review > New Comment, or Ctrl+Alt+M (InsertAnnotation; Cmd+Alt+M on Mac). A comment card appears in the right margin / Comments pane anchored to the selected text. The selected text gets a colored highlight (the "anchor"). The user types into the card and explicitly POSTS it (Post comment button or Ctrl+Enter) — deliberate posting so colleagues don't see drafts. Each posted card shows: author name, a relative timestamp/date stamp, the comment text, a pencil (edit own comment), a three-dot "More thread actions" menu (Delete / Resolve thread / Link to comment / Copy link), and a Reply field. There is also an "Assign to" checkbox (task) — out of scope here.

DELETE (split button): Click a comment, Review > Delete removes the selected comment. The dropdown arrow offers: "Delete" (current), "Delete All Comments Shown" (deletes only comments currently visible given the markup filter), and "Delete All Comments in Document" (removes every comment).

PREVIOUS / NEXT: Review > Next selects/scrolls to the next comment; Previous goes to the prior one. Navigation wraps: Next on the last comment returns to the first; Previous on the first goes to the last.

SHOW COMMENTS (toggle): Toggles between Contextual view (cards float in the right margin near their anchor; resolved comments are HIDDEN) and the List view (a dedicated Comments pane on the far right showing ALL comments including resolved). Greyed out unless Display-for-Review is Simple Markup (in our clone we ignore that dependency and just toggle the pane). The Comments pane has a "New" button at top.

MARKUP/COLOR: With "All Markup", anchor highlights show on the canvas. Multiple reviewers are color-coded — each author/user gets a distinct color so you can see who wrote what. Selecting a card highlights its anchor and scrolls to it; hovering a card emphasizes its anchor (and vice-versa). Resolved threads stay out of the margin but remain findable in the Comments pane; reopening a resolved comment makes it visible again.

ACCESSIBILITY: Ctrl+Alt+M to insert; Ctrl+Enter to post; screen-reader support for navigating/reading/deleting comments. Cards are keyboard-focusable.
specs.webFindings:
Sources used:
- Insert/delete/change a comment: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/insert-delete-or-change-a-comment-5cb1af25-4dfe-4484-9713-2c80391ecf12 (New Comment via Review tab; Delete; Delete via the arrow > "Delete All Comments in Document"; Next to select comments).
- Using Modern comments in Word: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/using-modern-comments-in-word-edc6ae71-0a2d-49fe-8faa-986f1e48136a (Ctrl+Alt+M; Post comment / Ctrl+Enter; card layout = author, timestamp, pencil edit, three-dot More thread actions = delete/resolve/link, Reply field, Assign to; Contextual vs List view; resolved hidden in contextual; All Markup anchor highlights; Comments pane far right with New button; selecting a comment highlights anchor and scrolls).
- Modern Comments overview: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/modern-comments-in-word-53d8b54e-5036-40f8-9a09-3271dea169ba and techcommunity intro: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft_365blog/introducing-modern-comments-in-microsoft-word/2263182 (modern anchoring, no dotted leader lines).
- Keyboard shortcuts: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/keyboard-shortcuts-in-word-95ef89dd-7142-4b50-afb2-f762f663ceb2 (Ctrl+Alt+M = InsertAnnotation).
- Screen-reader comments: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/use-a-screen-reader-to-add-read-and-delete-comments-in-word-c4648d50-4f7b-4795-b487-9e3141a5a596.
- Reviewer color coding (multiple authors shown in different colors): https://www.ionos.com/digitalguide/online-marketing/online-sales/how-to-insert-comments-in-word/

CONCRETE DATA EXTRACTED: shortcut Ctrl+Alt+M (post Ctrl+Enter); split items exactly "Delete" / "Delete All Comments Shown" / "Delete All Comments in Document" (already in ribbon-data.js); Previous/Next wrap-around; Contextual (resolved hidden) vs List (all) views; per-author color coding; card fields author+timestamp+text+reply+resolve+edit+delete.
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes — a right-docked Comments task pane plus in-document anchor highlights and per-comment card UI.

1) COMMENTS PANE (reuse .taskpane.right pattern from dialogs.js navPane/stylesPane). id="comments-pane", appended to #workarea. Header: title "Comments" + a "New" button + close X (WC.icon('win_close')). Body: vertically stacked comment cards. Each card: a left color bar in the author's color; author name (bold) + relative timestamp ("Just now", "2m ago", or date); comment text; a Reply input row; a three-dot menu (flyout: Edit, Resolve/Reopen, Delete, Copy link); resolved cards rendered dimmed with a "Resolved" chip. Clicking a card selects it (adds .selected), highlights+scrolls its anchor; hovering a card adds emphasis to its anchor.

2) NEW-COMMENT CARD: opening New Comment scrolls the pane to a fresh editable card with a textarea, Post (Ctrl+Enter) and Cancel buttons; the anchor span is created immediately (pending state) and finalized/removed on Post/Cancel.

3) ANCHOR HIGHLIGHT in #editor: span.wc-comment[data-cid][data-author][data-resolved] with background highlight in the author color (~25% alpha) and a subtle underline; .wc-comment.active for the currently-selected/hovered anchor (stronger highlight). Collapsed-caret comments insert a zero-width anchor marker so a point comment still has a location.

4) New CSS (add to dialogs.css or editor.css): .wc-comment / .wc-comment.active / .wc-comment.resolved; .cmt-card, .cmt-card.selected, .cmt-card.resolved, .cmt-author, .cmt-time, .cmt-bar (color), .cmt-text, .cmt-reply, .cmt-menu, .cmt-chip. Author color palette of ~8 Word-like reviewer colors assigned round-robin by author.

5) Show Comments toggle styling reuses the existing .toggled ribbon class (like trackChanges at commands.js:153).
specs.implementationPlan:
FILES: src/renderer/js/dialogs.js (replace WC.Comments), src/renderer/js/commands.js (handlers + dispatch), src/renderer/js/ribbon-data.js (fix cmd collisions), src/renderer/js/app.js (Ctrl+Alt+M), src/renderer/styles/dialogs.css or editor.css (styles). No new files.

A) FIX RIBBON CMD COLLISIONS (ribbon-data.js, ~lines 2211-2247). The Comments group currently uses generic cmds delete/previous/next which COLLIDE with the Changes group's previous/next (2349/2357) and a generic delete; controlIndex is keyed by c.cmd (ribbon.js:230) and dispatch is by cmd (commands.js:408). Rename the Comments-group controls' cmds to deleteComment, previousComment, nextComment (keep their stable ids review.comments.*). Leave the split "items" array as-is. This is mandatory or Comments nav/delete will fire the Changes handlers (or notImplemented).

B) DATA MODEL (in WC.Comments). State held in DOM as the single source of truth so getHTML/setHTML persistence is free (getHTML keeps comment spans). Each comment = anchor span in #editor:
  span.wc-comment with data-cid (uid), data-author, data-ts (ISO), data-text (the comment body), data-resolved ("1"|absent), optional data-replies (JSON array of {author,ts,text}). WC.Comments keeps only transient runtime bits: currentAuthor (from a name, default "Author"), authorColors map, selectedCid. Helper list(): Array.from(editor.querySelectorAll('span.wc-comment[data-cid]')) in document order. colorFor(author): assign from an 8-color palette round-robin, memoized.

C) WC.Comments API (replace lines 542-553 in dialogs.js):
  - add(): if selection non-collapsed in editor, surroundContents a new span.wc-comment (as today) but with data-cid/author/ts and EMPTY data-text (pending); if collapsed, insert a zero-width anchor span at caret. Open the pane, render, and focus a new editable card for this cid (draft mode). saveRange first. Set E().dirty.
  - post(cid, text): write data-text, exit draft, render. cancel(cid): if data-text empty, unwrap the span (replace with its children) and render.
  - remove(cid): unwrap that span (keep its text in the doc), render; if pane empty show empty state.
  - removeAllShown(): remove all non-resolved (contextual-visible) comments. removeAllInDocument(): remove every wc-comment span.
  - resolve(cid)/reopen(cid): toggle data-resolved; in contextual view resolved cards hide from margin but stay in pane list.
  - reply(cid, text): push to data-replies JSON. edit(cid, text): rewrite data-text.
  - select(cid): set selectedCid, add .active to that anchor (remove from others), scrollIntoView the anchor, add .selected to its card.
  - next()/previous(): compute index of selectedCid in list(); move +1/-1 with wrap-around (last->first, first->last); select() the result. If none selected, pick first/last.
  - openPane()/closePane()/togglePane(): create/remove #comments-pane (reuse navPane structure). render() rebuilds cards. showComments() flips a view mode flag (contextual vs list) AND ensures the pane is open; toggle the ribbon button .toggled.
  - exists(): true if any wc-comment present (used to enable/disable nav/delete and to gate notImplemented).

D) COMMANDS (commands.js handlers, register near line 149-153 Review section):
  H.newComment = () => WC.Comments.add();  (already present at line 85 — keep; it currently calls add()).
  H.deleteComment = (c, node) => WC.Commands.dropdown(c, node) when split arrow, but the main button = delete selected: implement H.deleteComment to delete the selected/current comment, and add a dropdown branch.
  H.previousComment = () => WC.Comments.previous();
  H.nextComment = () => WC.Comments.next();
  H.showComments = (c, node) => { WC.Comments.showComments(); node && node.classList.toggle('toggled', WC.Comments.paneOpen()); }  (manual toggle like trackChanges, NOT via TOGGLE_MAP/queryState).
  Dispatch: in Commands.dropdown add: if (cmd === 'deleteComment') return commentDeleteMenu(node); where commentDeleteMenu builds a flyout with WC.flyItem 'Delete' -> Comments.remove(selected), 'Delete All Comments Shown' -> removeAllShown(), 'Delete All Comments in Document' -> removeAllInDocument(). Also Commands.run already routes split w/o handler to dropdown, but since we add H.deleteComment for the primary click, keep H.deleteComment = delete-selected and route the arrow separately (the ribbon split passes the arrow click through dropdown()).

E) SHORTCUT (app.js bindKeys ~line 80): add `if (e.altKey && k === 'm') return () => WC.Comments.add();` (Ctrl+Alt+M). Also allow Ctrl+Enter inside a draft card's textarea to post (handled locally in the card, not the global map).

F) ANCHOR-EDITOR SYNC: clicking a .wc-comment span in the editor selects its card (add a delegated click listener in editor.js init or in Comments.openPane). Keep editor.getHTML() as-is (it already preserves comment spans; the zero-width char strip won't hurt). No change to repaginate.

G) DOCUMENTATION: Translate/Researcher/Linked Notes remain WC.notImplemented (cloud features) — no work here beyond ensuring they still route to notImplemented; mention in docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md if that file is being maintained.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (Electron app, Review tab) — no test runner present in this repo:
1) Select a word, click New Comment (and separately test Ctrl+Alt+M): a yellow/author-colored highlight wraps the selection, the right Comments pane opens with a draft card focused. Type text, press Ctrl+Enter (and separately click Post): card shows author + timestamp + text; highlight persists.
2) Collapsed caret + New Comment: a point anchor is created and a card appears.
3) Add 3 comments. Click Next repeatedly: selection cycles c1->c2->c3->c1 (wrap). Click Previous: cycles backwards c1->c3 (wrap). Each step scrolls the anchor into view and marks the card .selected and the anchor .active.
4) Click an anchor span in the document: its card becomes selected. Click a card: its anchor scrolls into view + highlights.
5) Delete (main button) with a comment selected: that one comment's span is unwrapped (text remains), card removed. Delete arrow > Delete All Comments Shown: only unresolved comments removed. Delete arrow > Delete All Comments in Document: all removed, pane shows empty state.
6) Resolve a comment via the card three-dot menu: in contextual view it leaves the margin but stays listed; Reopen restores it.
7) Reply to a comment: reply text stored (persists through save/load).
8) Show Comments toggles the ribbon button .toggled state and opens/closes the pane.
9) Multi-author color: set a different author name, add a comment; verify a distinct color bar/highlight.
10) Persistence round-trip: Save (getHTML) then Open/setHTML the document; comments (text, author, timestamp, resolved, replies) survive because all state is on data-* of the anchor span. After load, open the pane and confirm all cards render.
11) Regression: confirm the Changes group's Previous/Next (cmd previousChange-context) still works and is NOT hijacked by the comment nav after the cmd rename; confirm trackChanges toggle unaffected.
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible — fully client-side, no backend. The ribbon already declares the entire Comments group with the correct split items, and WC.Comments.add already proves selection-wrapping works. All needed primitives exist: WC.Editor.saveRange/restoreRange/getHTML/setHTML, .taskpane.right pane pattern (navPane/stylesPane), WC.dialog/flyout/flyItem/toast, WC.el with dataset+on* support, and the .toggled ribbon class (mirrored from trackChanges). Persistence is free because getHTML preserves comment spans and all state lives on data-* attributes.

GOTCHAS TO HANDLE (load-bearing):
1) CMD COLLISION (mandatory fix): Comments group cmds delete/previous/next collide with the Changes group (previous/next at ribbon-data.js 2349/2357) and a generic delete. Since controlIndex and dispatch key purely on cmd, rename the Comments controls to deleteComment/previousComment/nextComment, or comment nav will trigger Changes handlers / notImplemented.
2) Show Comments must be toggled manually (like trackChanges at commands.js:153), NOT through TOGGLE_MAP/queryState which only covers execCommand states.
3) surroundContents throws on partial-node selections — keep the existing try/extractContents+insertNode fallback.
4) styleWithCSS+execCommand can strip classes; use direct DOM (insertNodeHTML pattern) for anchor spans, not execCommand('insertHTML').
5) The existing prompt()-based add() should be replaced by the in-pane draft card to match modern "deliberate post" UX and avoid a blocking prompt.

Effort: moderate (one self-contained WC.Comments module rewrite + ~4 small handlers + ribbon cmd rename + CSS). Translate/Researcher/Linked Notes stay documented-not-implemented (cloud).
apis: WC.Editor.saveRange / restoreRange (selection persistence for anchoring)
WC.Editor.node / getHTML / setHTML (DOM source-of-truth + persistence round-trip)
window.getSelection() + Range.surroundContents/extractContents/insertNode (wrap selection as anchor span)
WC.el (dataset + on* event props)
WC.dialog / WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flyHeader / WC.flySep (delete split menu + card three-dot menu)
WC.toast / WC.notImplemented
WC.icon('win_close') (pane close button)
WC.Commands.run / WC.Commands.dropdown / handler map H (commands.js dispatch)
ribbon controlIndex keyed by cmd + .toggled class + syncToggles (Show Comments button state)
.taskpane.right CSS pattern (#workarea-docked comments pane)
app.js bindKeys global keydown map (Ctrl+Alt+M)

specs.feature: Review tab — Proofing + Accessibility + Language groups: Editor/Spelling & Grammar (OS spellcheck toggle + suggestions stub), Thesaurus (synonym lookup for the selected word), Word Count (already done), Check Accessibility (real linter with a results task pane), Translate (Translate Selection / Translate Document — documented cloud stub), and Language (Set Proofing Language — real; Language Preferences — documented).
specs.wordBehavior:
How real Microsoft Word behaves (verified against support.microsoft.com):

EDITOR / SPELLING & GRAMMAR (F7): In Microsoft 365 the Editor pane opens on the right and lists spelling, grammar, and "refinement" (style) suggestions with a score; classic Word 2021 shows a Spelling & Grammar dialog. Misspellings get red squiggles, grammar gets blue/double-underline. In this clone the squiggles are drawn by the OS via the contenteditable spellcheck attribute; the suggestions panel is approximated.

THESAURUS (Shift+F7): Opens a Thesaurus task pane on the right. You select/right-click a word, it shows grouped synonyms (and antonyms) organized by sense/part of speech, each with a meaning label. Clicking Insert (or the dropdown ▸ Insert) replaces the selected word; there is also Copy and Look Up. Word's data comes from a bundled thesaurus + the cloud Bing dictionary.

WORD COUNT: Already done (Pages, Words, Characters w/ and w/o spaces, Paragraphs, Lines).

CHECK ACCESSIBILITY: Opens an "Accessibility" task pane on the right with an Inspection Results tree grouped by severity — Errors, Warnings, Tips — plus (in 365) "Intelligent Services" suggestions. Each result group lists the offending objects; selecting one selects it in the document and shows a "Why Fix" explanation + "Steps To Fix" + recommended actions at the bottom of the pane. The status bar can show "Accessibility: Investigate" / "Accessibility: Good to go". Official rule classifications (verified):
ERRORS — Alt Text for non-text content (all images/objects must have alt text that is not just the filename/extension); Table has header row/column specified; Form fields have titles (Word); Document access not restricted by IRM.
WARNINGS — Use simple table structure (no merged/split/nested cells); Sufficient text-to-background contrast; Media has closed captions.
TIPS — Use heading styles / a Table of Contents to give the document structure (also: headings should be in logical order without skipping levels per the authoring guidance).
INTELLIGENT SERVICES — AI-generated alt text needs manual review.

TRANSLATE: A cloud feature (Microsoft Translator / Office Intelligent Services, requires sign-in + internet; ~62 languages). The first use prompts "Turn On" intelligent services. Two commands: Translate Selection opens the Translator pane with a Selection tab (auto-detect source → choose target → Insert replaces selection); Translate Document opens the Document tab and produces a NEW translated copy of the whole document with formatting/links intact, leaving the original unchanged.

LANGUAGE: Two menu items. Set Proofing Language… opens a dialog where you (a) mark the selected text / whole doc with a language from a scrollable list (languages above a double line are dictionary-installed), (b) toggle "Do not check spelling or grammar", and (c) toggle "Detect language automatically" (needs a full sentence to work). Setting it changes which dictionary proofs the text. Language Preferences… opens File ▸ Options ▸ Language (editing languages, display/Help language, proofing language) — out of scope as a real settings store.
specs.webFindings:
Authoritative Microsoft sources used:

- Rules for the Accessibility Checker (the exact Error/Warning/Tip classification of every rule, incl. Alt Text, Table Column Headers, Simple Table Structure, Text-Background Contrast, Heading Styles tip, AI-generated alt text): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/rules-for-the-accessibility-checker-651e08f2-0fc3-4e10-aaca-74b4a67101c1

- Make your Word documents accessible (alt text on all images/non-text or mark decorative; built-in heading styles in logical order without skipping levels; simple table with a designated header row; descriptive hyperlink text): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/make-your-word-documents-accessible-to-people-with-disabilities-d9bf3683-87ac-47ea-b91a-78dcacb3c66d

- Set Proofing Language / automatic detection (Review ▸ Language ▸ Set Proofing Language; "Detect language automatically"; "Do not check spelling or grammar"; languages above the double line are the auto-detectable installed dictionaries): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/check-spelling-and-grammar-in-a-different-language-667ba67a-a202-42fd-8596-edc1fa320e00 and https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/turn-on-automatic-language-detection-194d309c-3e10-4bd5-a694-ba9bbdf4a28e

- Translate text into a different language (Translate Selection vs Translate Document; Translator pane Selection/Document tabs; Microsoft Translator cloud service; ~62 languages; requires Office intelligent services + internet; "Turn On" prompt on first use): https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/translate-text-into-a-different-language-287380e4-a56c-48a1-9977-f2dca89ce93f and https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/business/word/

Accessibility status (color/severity) maps directly to a results-pane grouped by Errors / Warnings / Tips. Standard Word comment/markup accent color is its usual review red/orange, but that is not needed here (Comments/Tracking are separate groups already partly wired).
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes — UI is required for several items. All should reuse the existing toolkit (no new framework):

1) Accessibility task pane (NEW) — class 'taskpane right', id 'a11y-pane', appended to #workarea, toggle-by-id pattern (re-click removes it). Head: WC.icon('win_close') close X + title "Accessibility". Body: a status line ("Good to go" green check, or "Investigate" with N issues), then collapsible groups Errors / Warnings / Tips, each listing result rows. Each row: rule label + object preview; clicking it scrolls the offending node into view and outlines it (reuse scrollIntoView like navPane; add a temporary .a11y-target outline). Bottom region: "Why Fix" + "Steps To Fix" text for the selected rule, plus a Recommended Action button where automatic (e.g. "Add Alt Text…", "Mark as header row"). A "Re-scan" button. Mirror the visual structure of D.navPane / D.stylesPane.

2) Thesaurus task pane (NEW) — class 'taskpane right', id 'thesaurus-pane'. Head with search input (prefilled with selected word) + title. Body: grouped synonym list (sense label as a sub-header, synonyms as clickable rows). Each row hover shows an Insert affordance; clicking inserts/replaces the selected word in the document. Falls back to an honest empty-state message when the word is not in the built-in map.

3) Set Proofing Language dialog (NEW) — WC.dialog({title:'Language', width:'420px', ...}). Body: a scrollable single-select language list (a curated ~25-language list with the common dictionary ones at top above a hairline divider), a "Do not check spelling or grammar" checkbox, and a "Detect language automatically" checkbox. Footer OK/Cancel. OK writes lang + spellcheck onto the target (selection block(s) or #editor).

4) Editor / Spelling pane — already exists (D.editorPane, id 'editor-pane'); only minor wording. Spelling & Grammar toggle already exists (runSpellCheck).

5) Translate — a small Translator task pane stub (id 'translate-pane') with Selection/Document tab chrome, a target-language dropdown, and an honest "cloud service" explanation + a "Turn On"-style note; OR a toast documenting the cloud limitation. Prefer the pane chrome for fidelity, with the actual translate action disabled/stubbed.

Reuse existing CSS classes: taskpane / taskpane.right, tp-head, tp-title, tp-body, tp-result, info-props, row, btn/btn.primary, dlg-body. Add a few small style rules (a11y severity colors, divider) to the existing stylesheet rather than inline-only where it aids clarity.
specs.implementationPlan:
All work is in /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/ . No new files required (add functions to existing modules). Files: dialogs.js (panes + dialog), commands.js (handlers + dropdown wiring), and a few CSS rules in src/renderer/styles/. A) Wire the dispatcher (commands.js): - Add handlers near line 149 (the "// ---- Review ----" block): H.thesaurus = () => WC.Dialogs.thesaurusPane(); H.checkAccessibility = () => WC.Dialogs.accessibilityPane(); - For the split/dropdown controls (translate, language, checkAccessibility split), add explicit branches inside Commands.dropdown() (around line 438, alongside the existing per-tab branches): if (cmd === 'checkAccessibility') return accessibilityMenu(node); // items: Check Accessibility / Alt Text / Accessibility Reminder / Options… if (cmd === 'translate') return translateMenu(node); // Translate Selection / Translate Document if (cmd === 'language') return languageMenu(node); // Set Proofing Language… / Language Preferences… Each helper builds a WC.flyout with WC.flyItem rows whose onClick calls the right pane/dialog; the primary-click (H.checkAccessibility) opens the pane directly. Keep Language Preferences… and the Translate actions as documented stubs (WC.toast with the cloud/limitation note). B) Accessibility Checker — REAL linter (dialogs.js, new D.accessibilityPane + a pure WC.A11y.scan(root) function): Scan WC.Editor.node and produce {errors:[], warnings:[], tips:[]} using these concrete DOM rules (mirrors Word's classification): ERRORS: - Missing/empty alt text: every whose alt attribute is missing, empty, or equals its filename/src basename. Object preview = thumbnail or filename. Recommended action: "Add Alt Text…" → small dialog writing img.alt (also allow "Mark as decorative" → alt=""). - Table missing header: every with no row. Recommended action: "Use first row as header" → convert first 's
and no
to (scope="col"). WARNINGS: - Complex table structure: any containing cells with colspan>1/rowspan>1 (merged) or a nested
. (No auto-fix; explain.) - Low contrast (best-effort): elements with an explicit color + background where computed luminance contrast < 4.5:1 (compute via getComputedStyle + WCAG relative-luminance formula). Honest note that it is heuristic. HEADING/STRUCTURE (Word puts the "use headings/TOC" item under Tips, and skipped-level under guidance): - Tip: document has no h1–h6 at all → "Use heading styles to add structure." - Warning/Tip: heading level jumps (e.g. h1 → h3 skips h2) → list each skip with the heading text. Each result carries {id, rule, severity, node, why, steps, fix?(fn)}. The pane renders groups, wires row-click to select+scroll+outline the node, and renders Why/Steps + the fix button. Re-scan button re-runs WC.A11y.scan. Status line: "Good to go" if all empty. C) Thesaurus (dialogs.js, new D.thesaurusPane + a built-in synonym map): - Add a compact WC.THESAURUS object: ~120–180 common headwords → { synonyms:[...], antonyms:[...] } (optionally grouped by sense). Lowercase keys; lookup strips punctuation and lowercases the selected word. - Pane: search box (prefilled from current selection word), grouped synonym rows. Clicking a synonym replaces the selected word: re-select the word via WC.Editor.restoreRange (if a single word is selected) or, if only the caret is in a word, expand to the word boundary, then E().insertHTML(synonym). Preserve capitalization of the original (Title-case if original was capitalized). - Empty state: honest "No synonyms found in the built-in thesaurus for ''. Word's full thesaurus uses a bundled lexicon + cloud dictionary." Keep it as the documented partial. D) Set Proofing Language (dialogs.js, new D.proofingLanguage): - LANGS list with {name, code} (e.g. English (United States) en-US, English (UK) en-GB, French fr-FR, German de-DE, Spanish es-ES, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Russian, Turkish tr-TR, Arabic, Chinese, Japanese, etc.), with the "installed/auto-detectable" ones rendered above a divider. - Targets: selected block(s) via E().selectedBlocks() if a non-collapsed selection/blocks exist, else the whole #editor. On OK: set lang attribute (e.g. node.setAttribute('lang', code)) and node.spellcheck/setAttribute('spellcheck', ...) honoring "Do not check spelling or grammar" (spellcheck=false + a 'no-proof' class), and reflect "Detect language automatically" by toggling a WC.Editor-level flag + clearing explicit lang when on. Default the dialog's selected radio to the current target's lang. - This is genuinely functional: it changes #editor/@lang so the OS proofing dictionary switches, matching Word's real effect on squiggles. E) Translate (commands.js/dialogs.js — documented cloud stub with pane chrome): - translateMenu: "Translate Selection" / "Translate Document" → open D.translatePane(mode) (a 'taskpane right' #translate-pane with Selection/Document tabs, From=Auto-detect, To=, and a disabled "Translate" / explanatory body) OR a WC.toast documenting the cloud requirement. Add a NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md line clarifying it is a cloud stub. F) Status bar (optional, statusbar.js): add an "Accessibility: Good to go / Investigate" indicator that runs WC.A11y.scan on document change (debounced) and opens the pane on click — matches Word. Only if low-risk. Module exposure: attach WC.A11y = { scan }, extend WC.Dialogs with accessibilityPane/thesaurusPane/proofingLanguage/translatePane, and WC.THESAURUS / WC.LANGS data tables. CSS additions go in the existing stylesheet under src/renderer/styles/.
specs.testPlan:
Manual + light automated checks (Electron renderer, vanilla JS — exercise WC.* in the devtools console / a small harness): Accessibility Checker: - Build a doc with: an with no alt, an (filename), a clean , a with no
, a with a merged cell (colspan=2), a doc with h1 then h3 (skipped h2), and a doc with no headings. Run WC.A11y.scan(editor) and assert each lands in the right bucket (alt errors, table-header error, complex-table warning, heading-skip + no-heading tips). Verify the clean img and a proper header table produce NO findings. - Open the pane: verify groups render with counts, clicking a result scrolls+outlines the node, "Add Alt Text…" writes img.alt and the result disappears on Re-scan, "Use first row as header" converts
→ and clears the error. Verify "Good to go" state when the doc is clean. Thesaurus: - Select a word present in the map (e.g. "happy") → pane lists synonyms; clicking one replaces the selected word in #editor and preserves capitalization ("Happy" → "Glad"). Select a word not in the map → honest empty-state message. Select nothing → prompt to select a word. Set Proofing Language: - Put caret/selection in a paragraph, open dialog, pick French, OK → that block gets lang="fr-FR"; pick "Do not check spelling or grammar" → spellcheck disabled on the target (squiggles stop). Reopen dialog → current language preselected. With whole-doc (no selection) → #editor @lang updates. Verify "Detect language automatically" toggles the flag and does not crash. Spelling & Grammar / Editor: - Toggle spellingGrammar flips #editor @spellcheck true/false (already works); Editor pane opens/closes and shows live counts. Translate / Language Preferences: - Translate Selection/Document opens the documented Translator pane (or toast) and does not throw; Language Preferences shows the documented message. Regression: confirm Word Count still works, the Review-tab buttons no longer fall through to generic notImplemented, and opening/closing each new pane via re-click toggles cleanly (no duplicate panes, no leftover outlines). Sanity-load the app (npm start / electron) and click every Review ▸ Proofing/Accessibility/Language control.
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible in one session. The Accessibility Checker, Thesaurus, and Set Proofing Language are fully real, local, deterministic DOM operations that fit the existing contenteditable + task-pane + dialog patterns exactly — no cloud needed. Concrete points:

REAL (build fully):
- Accessibility Checker: pure DOM lint over #editor (img alt, table th/thead, merged/nested cells, heading-level order, presence of headings). Classifications taken verbatim from Microsoft's "Rules for the Accessibility Checker" page. Auto-fixes (add alt, promote header row) are trivial DOM edits. Contrast check is a best-effort heuristic (computed-style luminance) and should be labeled as such — that is the only soft spot and it matches Word's own "review visually" caveat.
- Thesaurus: a hand-built synonym map (~150 headwords) is honest and works offline; replacement uses the existing selection/insertHTML plumbing. Limitation: coverage is small vs Word's lexicon — document it.
- Set Proofing Language: genuinely functional because it sets @lang/@spellcheck on the target, which actually redirects the OS proofing dictionary — same observable effect as Word.

DOCUMENTED CLOUD STUBS (honest, per existing NOT_IMPLEMENTED policy):
- Translate (Translate Selection/Document): requires Microsoft Translator / Office Intelligent Services + internet/sign-in — render the Translator pane chrome (Selection/Document tabs, language picker) but keep the translate action a documented stub. Add a NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md line.
- Editor pane full suggestion/rewrite UI and grammar/style critique: stays approximated (OS squiggles only) — already documented.
- Language Preferences (File ▸ Options ▸ Language settings store): documented stub.
- Researcher and Linked Notes (OneNote) are separate cloud features already listed as not implemented; leave as documented.

Dispatcher wiring is the only "gotcha": thesaurus/checkAccessibility/translate/language currently have NO H[] handler so they fall through to WC.notImplemented; the split/dropdown items also need explicit branches in Commands.dropdown() (around line 438) or they each toast notImplemented. Both are small, well-understood edits. Overall: low risk, high fidelity.
apis: DOM/contenteditable (document.querySelectorAll, getComputedStyle, Element.scrollIntoView, setAttribute lang/spellcheck) — already used throughout editor.js
window.getSelection / Range — for Thesaurus word replacement and proofing-language targets (existing E().saveRange/restoreRange/selectedBlocks)
WC.dialog / WC.toast / WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flySep / WC.icon / WC.escapeHtml — existing in-house UI toolkit (util.js)
Existing taskpane CSS classes (taskpane.right, tp-head, tp-title, tp-body, tp-result) — reuse for the new panes
WCAG relative-luminance / 4.5:1 contrast formula — implemented inline for the best-effort contrast warning (no external lib)
Built-in static data tables added in-repo: WC.THESAURUS (synonym map) and WC.LANGS (proofing-language list) — no network

DOCUMENTED-ONLY (not called, stubbed): Microsoft Translator / Office Intelligent Services cloud API for Translate; File▸Options Language settings for Language Preferences — surfaced as honest stubs per docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md

specs.feature: Review tab — Compare + Protect + Ink + OneNote groups: (1) Compare → Compare two versions (legal blackline) and Combine revisions; (2) Restrict Editing pane (real read-only toggle + formatting/editing restriction UI); (3) Block Authors (documented cloud stub with a real visual "blocked region" approximation); (4) Hide Ink (real toggle of the .ink-layer visibility); (5) Linked Notes / OneNote (documented cloud stub).
specs.wordBehavior: COMPARE (Review > Compare group): "Compare two versions of a document (legal blackline)" opens a dialog with Original document and Revised document pickers, plus options (comparison granularity: character-level vs word-level; what to compare: text, formatting, comments, etc.) and "Show changes in: New document / Original / Revised". Word produces a NEW third document in which the original's tracked changes are accepted and the differences from original→revised are rendered AS tracked changes (insertions + deletions). Source docs are never modified. "Combine" merges revisions from multiple authors/reviewers into one document, attributing each reviewer's tracked changes. TRACK-CHANGES MARKUP (the rendering target): insertions = underline, deletions = strikethrough, color = "By author" (Word auto-assigns a distinct color per reviewer; can be forced per-markup-type). All Markup view shows ins/del inline in author colors. RESTRICT EDITING (Review > Protect group): opens the Restrict Editing task pane on the right with three sections — (1) Formatting restrictions ("Limit formatting to a selection of styles"); (2) Editing restrictions ("Allow only this type of editing in the document") with a dropdown: No changes (Read only) / Tracked changes / Comments / Filling in forms, plus an Exceptions area to exempt selected ranges/users; (3) "Yes, Start Enforcing Protection" which (optionally) sets a password and locks the document into the chosen mode (Read only makes the body non-editable). BLOCK AUTHORS (Review > Protect group): in a SharePoint/OneDrive co-authoring session, select content and click Block Authors to stop other co-authors editing that selection; the blocked range shows a dotted border on its left edge. Releasable at any time. Cloud/co-auth dependent. HIDE INK (Review > Ink group): a toggle that hides/shows the ink annotation layer (the strokes from the Draw tab) in the document; appears when the doc contains ink or on pen/touch devices. LINKED NOTES (Review > OneNote group): launches OneNote docking and links notes you take to the part of the Word doc you're working on; requires OneNote integration (cloud/desktop OneNote).
specs.webFindings: Confirmed via Microsoft Support and secondary sources. Compare/legal blackline: support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/compare-document-differences-using-the-legal-blackline-option (dbfc7351-...) — Original + Revised pickers, character vs word level, Show changes in New/Original/Revised, output is a new doc with original's changes accepted and revised diffs shown as tracked changes; source docs unchanged. Combine vs Compare distinction confirmed (legalofficeguru.com). Restrict Editing pane structure confirmed (erinwrightwriting.com, geeksforgeeks.org): Formatting restrictions = "Limit formatting to a selection of styles"; Editing restrictions = "Allow only this type of editing" dropdown with No changes (Read only) / Tracked changes / Comments / Filling in forms; Exceptions area for Comments/No-changes; "Yes, Start Enforcing Protection" with optional password. Block Authors confirmed (oreilly Word 2013 on Demand; efficiency365.com; support.microsoft.com co-authoring docs): select content, Review > Protect > Block Authors, blocked area gets a dotted border on the left, requires SharePoint/co-authoring, releasable anytime. Track Changes markup colors confirmed (support.microsoft.com change-the-track-changes-color 48c80bb3-...): insertions default = Underline, deletions default = Strikethrough, color = "By author" (auto distinct color per reviewer; can be overridden per markup type). Hide Ink / OneNote Linked Notes are device/cloud features (bettersolutions.com Review-tab reference; OneNote support docs). Sources: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/compare-document-differences-using-the-legal-blackline-option-dbfc7351-4022-43a2-a0c4-54d1898702a0 , https://legalofficeguru.com/compare-vs-combine-difference/ , https://erinwrightwriting.com/restrict-editing-in-microsoft-word/ , https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/ms-word/restricting-editing-in-ms-word/ , https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/word-2013-on/9780133353204/ch18lev1sec17.html , https://efficiency365.com/2015/07/22/blocking-others-from-editing-parts-of-the-document/ , https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/change-the-track-changes-color-48c80bb3-458c-4abb-8e21-25f9bac47484 , https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/track-changes-in-word-197ba630-0f5f-4a8e-9a77-3712475e806a , https://bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/review-tab.htm
specs.uiNeeded: 1) COMPARE DIALOG (use WC.dialog): title "Compare Documents". Body rows — Original document: read-only text input + "Browse…" button; Revised document: input + "Browse…"; radio group "Show changes: Character level / Word level"; radio group "Show changes in: New document (default) / Original / Revised". Footer: OK (primary) / Cancel. Reuse the same dialog shell for "Combine Documents" (title differs; second picker labeled "Revised/author document"). Browse buttons call window.wordAPI.open() (native picker already returns {ok,html,name,path}). 2) COMPARE TOOLBAR (small inline bar shown after a compare completes, or a status toast): "Accept All" / "Reject All" buttons operating on the rendered ins/del. Minimal — can reuse WC.toast with a follow-up or a thin bar above the editor. 3) RESTRICT EDITING TASK PANE (right-docked, reuse existing .taskpane.right pattern like D.stylesPane/navPane): id "restrict-pane"; sections: (a) "1. Formatting restrictions" checkbox "Limit formatting to a selection of styles"; (b) "2. Editing restrictions" checkbox "Allow only this type of editing in the document" + a <select> [No changes (Read only), Tracked Changes, Comments, Filling in forms]; (c) "3. Start enforcement" button "Yes, Start Enforcing Protection" + optional password input; when enforcing, button becomes "Stop Protection". Close X in tp-head. 4) RIBBON STATES: restrictEditing/hideInk/blockAuthors buttons get .toggled class via existing markChecked/classList.toggle pattern; Hide Ink reflects current ink-hidden state. No new CSS framework needed — add ~6 small CSS rules (ins/del markup colors, .wc-blocked dotted-left-border, .ink-hidden { display:none } on .ink-layer, a thin compare bar). All dialog/pane/flyout/toast primitives already exist (WC.dialog, .taskpane.right, WC.flyout/flyItem/flyHeader/flySep, WC.toast, WC.el, WC.icon).
specs.implementationPlan:
All work is renderer-side; NO main-process changes (wordAPI.open already does native-pick + returns {ok, html, name, path}). Wire the five unhandled Review cmds (compare, restrictEditing, blockAuthors, hideInk, linkedNotes) that currently fall through to WC.notImplemented.

FILE: src/renderer/js/commands.js — add handlers in the "// ---- Review ----" block (after H.trackChanges, line ~153):
- H.compare = (c, node) => WC.Commands.dropdown(c, node)  // it's type:"dropdown" with items Compare.../Combine...; route the two items.
  In Commands.dropdown add: if (cmd === 'compare') return compareMenu(node);  -> compareMenu shows flyItem 'Compare Two Versions…' (calls WC.Review.compareDialog('compare')) and 'Combine Revisions…' (WC.Review.compareDialog('combine')).
- H.restrictEditing = (c, node) => WC.Review.restrictPane(node);
- H.blockAuthors = (c, node) => WC.Review.blockAuthors(node);
- H.hideInk = (c, node) => WC.Review.toggleHideInk(node);
- H.linkedNotes = () => WC.toast('Linked Notes docks OneNote and links notes to your document — needs OneNote + cloud integration; not available in this local clone.', 'See docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md');

NEW FILE: src/renderer/js/review-tools.js — define WC.Review = { ... } (mirror structure of references-tools.js / layout-tools.js; const E = () => WC.Editor). Add a <script> include in src/renderer/index.html next to the other tool files, and call any init from app.js init list if needed (most tool modules are pure objects, no init).

  WC.Review.compareDialog(mode): build the Compare/Combine dialog (WC.dialog). Hold orig={html,name} and rev={html,name}. Browse buttons: const r = await window.wordAPI.open(); if(r&&r.ok){ orig = {html:r.html, name:r.name}; input.value=r.name; }. Default the Original to the CURRENT document (E().getHTML(), name from WC.Files.name) so a one-file compare works. On OK: run diff and render.

  Diff engine (text-level, real): 
    - Parse each doc's HTML into an array of block elements (p,h1..h6,li,blockquote). For each pair of blocks do a WORD-level (or character-level per the radio) LCS/Myers diff of the tokenized text.
    - Implement a small LCS diff over token arrays (split on /(\s+)/ to keep spaces) returning ops: equal / insert / delete. No dependency needed (~40 lines). For block add/remove, treat unmatched blocks as whole insert/delete.
    - Render: equal -> plain text; insert -> <ins class="wc-ins" data-author="Author">…</ins>; delete -> <del class="wc-del" data-author="Author">…</del>. Wrap result in a fresh document and E().setHTML(rendered) when "Show changes in: New document" (default). This matches Word: original's accepted state + revised diffs as tracked changes (ins/del).
    - Set WC.trackChanges = true and toggle the Track Changes ribbon button so markup is visibly "on".
  Combine mode: same diff but author label taken from a prompt/each author; render multiple authors' ins/del with distinct data-author colors.
  Accept/Reject: provide WC.Review.acceptAll() (unwrap <ins>, remove <del>) and rejectAll() (remove <ins>, unwrap <del>) operating on E().node; expose via a thin bar or toast actions.

  WC.Review.restrictPane(node): create/toggle a right task pane (id 'restrict-pane', class 'taskpane right') exactly like D.navPane/D.stylesPane. Controls as in uiNeeded. Behavior:
    - select 'No changes (Read only)' + Start Enforcing -> E().node.setAttribute('contenteditable','false'); add app class 'restricted'; button -> 'Stop Protection'. Stop -> contenteditable='true', remove class. (Honor Draw mode: don't clobber if ink drawing is on.)
    - 'Tracked changes' -> set WC.trackChanges=true (+toggle ribbon btn) and keep editable.
    - 'Comments' -> read-only body but allow WC.Comments (document as approximation).
    - 'Filling in forms' -> document as approximation (toast).
    - 'Limit formatting to a selection of styles' -> store a flag WC.Review.formatLocked (document as visual approximation).
    - Optional password: store in-memory only (clearly a clone approximation; note in toast).
    - Mark the ribbon restrictEditing button .toggled while enforcing.

  WC.Review.blockAuthors(node): real visual approximation of the Word behavior — if there's a non-collapsed selection inside the editor, wrap it in <span class="wc-blocked" contenteditable="false"> (gives the dotted left border + makes it non-editable, matching Word's "others can't edit this" intent) and toast that true block-authors needs SharePoint/OneDrive co-authoring. If no selection, toast the cloud explanation. Provide release: clicking the button again with caret inside a wc-blocked span (or 'Release All') unwraps blocked spans. Toggle ribbon .toggled if any blocked spans exist.

  WC.Review.toggleHideInk(node): const layer = E().node.querySelector('.ink-layer'); toggle a state WC.Review.inkHidden; apply E().node.classList.toggle('ink-hidden', hidden) (CSS: #editor.ink-hidden .ink-layer{display:none}). Update ribbon .toggled via markChecked(node); toast 'Ink hidden/shown'. If no ink layer exists, toast 'No ink in this document.'.

FILE: src/renderer/styles/editor.css — add:
  #editor ins.wc-ins { text-decoration: underline; color: #2B579A; }  /* author 1 blue */
  #editor del.wc-del { text-decoration: line-through; color: #C00000; }  /* author colors via data-author -> nth-of-type or inline color */
  #editor .wc-blocked { border-left: 2px dotted #b0b0b0; padding-left: 4px; }
  #editor.ink-hidden .ink-layer { display: none; }
  (author colors: assign a small palette by data-author in JS via inline style for "By author" fidelity.)

FILE: src/renderer/js/files.js sanitize() — ALLOWED_ATTR/ADD_TAGS already permits class/style/data-*; ensure 'ins' and 'del' survive DOMPurify: add ADD_TAGS ['u','ins','del'] and data-author to ALLOW_DATA_ATTR (already true) so compared docs round-trip through save/open.

Update docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md to document Block Authors (co-auth/SharePoint), Linked Notes (OneNote), and the password/exceptions parts of Restrict Editing as approximations.
specs.testPlan:
Manual / behavioral (this clone has no unit harness for renderer modules beyond scripts/test-suite.js; follow existing manual-verification style):
1) COMPARE: New doc with "The quick brown fox." Click Review > Compare > Compare Two Versions. Original defaults to current doc; Browse a revised .docx (or paste) reading "The quick red fox jumps." -> OK. Expect editor shows: "The quick " + <del>brown</del><ins>red</ins> + " fox" + <ins> jumps</ins> + "." with underline (ins, blue) and strikethrough (del, red). Verify source isn't mutated until OK. Verify Track Changes ribbon button shows toggled.
2) ACCEPT/REJECT: Accept All -> dels removed, ins unwrapped -> "The quick red fox jumps." Reject All on a fresh compare -> back to original "The quick brown fox."
3) COMBINE: pick two author docs; verify each author's ins/del gets a distinct color via data-author.
4) RESTRICT EDITING — Read only: open pane, choose "No changes (Read only)", Start Enforcing -> try to type in editor: nothing inserts (contenteditable=false), app has 'restricted' class, button reads 'Stop Protection'. Stop -> typing works again. Choose 'Tracked changes' + enforce -> WC.trackChanges true, still editable.
5) BLOCK AUTHORS: select a phrase, click Block Authors -> phrase wrapped in wc-blocked with dotted left border and not editable; toast explains SharePoint requirement. Release -> phrase editable again.
6) HIDE INK: Draw a stroke via Draw tab (creates .ink-layer). Review > Hide Ink -> strokes disappear (#editor.ink-hidden), button toggled; click again -> reappear. With no ink -> toast 'No ink in this document.'
7) LINKED NOTES: click -> toast documents OneNote/cloud requirement (no crash).
8) ROUND-TRIP: after a compare, Save then Open -> verify ins/del/wc-blocked survive DOMPurify sanitize (add ins/del to ADD_TAGS). 
9) REGRESSION: confirm unhandled-cmd fallthrough is gone (cmds no longer hit WC.notImplemented). Run existing npm test / scripts/test-suite.js to ensure no load errors from the new review-tools.js script include.
specs.feasibility: Highly feasible, fully renderer-side, no main-process or new-dependency changes. The Review tab controls already exist in ribbon-data.js (cmds compare/restrictEditing/blockAuthors/hideInk/linkedNotes) but have NO handlers — they currently fall through to WC.notImplemented, so this is purely additive wiring. REAL behaviors: (1) Compare/Combine as a genuine text diff (self-contained LCS over word/char tokens, ~40 lines, no library) rendered as ins/del tracked-change markup — loads two real documents through the existing window.wordAPI.open() bridge which already returns {ok,html,name,path}; (2) Restrict Editing read-only is a real contenteditable=false toggle driven by a real right-docked task pane reusing the existing .taskpane.right pattern; (3) Hide Ink is a real toggle of the existing .ink-layer (draw-tools.js) via a CSS class. DOCUMENTED CLOUD STUBS (honest toasts pointing to docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md): Block Authors (real visual wc-blocked dotted-border + non-editable approximation, but true co-authoring needs SharePoint/OneDrive) and Linked Notes (OneNote integration). All UI primitives (WC.dialog, taskpane, flyout, toast, el, icon, markChecked, WC.trackChanges flag) already exist. Main effort: new src/renderer/js/review-tools.js (~250-300 lines) + ~6 commands.js handler lines + ~5 CSS rules + a sanitize() tag whitelist tweak + one index.html script include. Key correctness watch-outs: keep contenteditable toggling from fighting Draw mode (draw-tools also sets contenteditable); ensure DOMPurify keeps ins/del/data-author on save/open round-trip; assign "By author" colors per data-author for fidelity.
